summary: PR 2525 added a large C implementation of src/melee/pl/plbonus.c:fn_80039618, adjusted fn_80038700 with a scoped dont_inline pragma, rewrote digit extraction arithmetic in fn_8003CC84, and refined pl_804D6470_t field types in src/melee/pl/types.h. The automated report shows main/melee/pl/plbonus fn_80039618 improved from 0.00% to 99.67%, fn_8003CC84 reached 100%, and plbonus .data/.sdata2 reached 100%; no human review comments were loaded.

key_files.role: Implemented fn_80039618, added scoped dont_inline around fn_80038700, and changed arithmetic in fn_8003CC84.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: plbonus.c hunks for fn_80038700, fn_80039618, fn_8003CC84
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report for fn_80039618 and fn_8003CC84

key_files.role: Refined pl_804D6470_t padding/unknown ranges into typed threshold fields used by plbonus logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_804D6470_t x0, x8, xC, x10, x20, x50, x54, xF0, x160 field changes

what_changed: Replaced the placeholder comment for fn_80039618 with a full function body using Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2, Player_GetActionStats, Player_GetEntity, setFlag, pl_CalculateAverage, gm_* helpers, Player_GetFalls, and Player_GetKOsByPlayerIndex.

fn_80039618 introduced local aliases for plActionStats counters and pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t fields, then evaluates many bonus conditions using loops, switch cases, ratio comparisons, bitfield checks, and flag-setting calls.
Added '#pragma push', '#pragma dont_inline on', and '#pragma pop' around fn_80038700.
Changed fn_8003CC84 digit extraction from modulo expressions to subtraction-based arithmetic.
Changed several pl_804D6470_t fields from byte padding to typed float/u32/int fields at offsets used by the new plbonus code.
Automated report recorded 6 new matches including plbonus .data, plbonus .sdata2, and fn_8003CC84; fn_80039618 improved to 99.67% but was not reported as a 100% match.
smart_moves: Recovered typed fields in pl_804D6470_t instead of accessing thresholds through raw padding bytes.
Kept repeated counter scans as natural for-loops over attack/hit arrays rather than fully unrolling the 100-entry and range-based checks.
Used a switch over sparse action IDs in the 0x40..0x62 scan to accumulate the sub-sum for selected cases.
Used scoped pragma push/pop rather than leaving a file-wide dont_inline setting.
Made a small arithmetic rewrite in fn_8003CC84 that the automated report associates with that function reaching 100%.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a decompiled function repeatedly reads configuration thresholds from a global struct, recovering concrete field types in the header can make the function source much clearer than keeping byte padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_804D6470_t padding replaced by x0/x8/xC/x10/x20/x50/x54/xF0/x160 typed fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: Modulo-heavy decimal digit extraction may need to be rewritten as quotient-minus-product expressions for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8003CC84 temp_r3_3/temp_r0 changed from % expressions to subtraction expressions
text_corpus: automated report lists fn_8003CC84 as a new 100% match

decomp_lessons.lesson: Large scoring/bonus functions can often be reconstructed as a sequence of small condition blocks over cached stat counters, typed table fields, natural loops, and setFlag calls.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: automated report shows fn_80039618 improved from 0.00% to 99.67%

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for unresolved symbols such as fn_80039618, fn_80038700, pl_80038628, pl_80037B2C, and pl_804D6470.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function and global names in plbonus.c

naming_conventions.convention: Short local role names were used where roles are clear: table, stats, gobj, atk_counts, hit_counts, x358_counts, attacks_total, hits_total, x358_total.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local declarations at start of fn_80039618

naming_conventions.convention: Unknown struct fields stayed offset-style, such as x0_staleMoveTable, x904, xC60, xD04, xD0C, xDD1, and pl_804D6470_t x-prefixed fields.

evidence_refs: raw_diff: added #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop around fn_80038700
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: The PR evidence does not include an explicit rationale for this pragma or adjacent-function verification beyond the automated match report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(16) at the end of fn_80039618.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: PAD_STACK is present in the diff, but no explicit objdiff rationale is loaded; automated report shows fn_80039618 at 99.67%, not full match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used goto labels for early-exit checks inside otherwise natural loop blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: skip_only_low_moves and skip_combo_count labels in fn_80039618
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Supported as source in this PR, but not enough evidence to claim these gotos are the original authored form.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Rewrote digit extraction to avoid nested modulo operations.

review_feedback.summary: Report for GALE01 showed matched code +2980 bytes, matched data +456 bytes, 6 new matches, and fn_80039618 improvement from 0.00% to 99.67%.

Automated report supports matching progress, but it does not provide detailed objdiff rationale for PAD_STACK or dont_inline.

Automated report lists unrelated sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3AA7 match changes even though the changed-file list only includes plbonus.c and types.h; this record does not infer intent or direct causality for those unrelated units.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed global config fields can unlock cleaner plbonus matching code
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2525, pl_804D6470_t padding fields were replaced with typed threshold fields that fn_80039618 then used directly. This is a concrete example of the typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard in src/melee/pl.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80039618 accesses to pl_804D6470 fields

accepted_candidate_records.title: Subtraction-based digit extraction matched fn_8003CC84
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2525 changed temp_ret % 100 / 10 and temp_ret % 100 % 10 into expressions based on temp_ret - temp_r5 * 100; the automated report lists fn_8003CC84 as reaching 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: automated report new match for fn_8003CC84

source_update_candidates.title: pl_804D6470_t typed fields used by plbonus
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2525, src/melee/pl/types.h changed pl_804D6470_t offsets +0, +8, +C, +10, +20, +50, +54, +F0, and +160 from padding/unknown ranges to typed float/u32 fields used by src/melee/pl/plbonus.c:fn_80039618.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may be useful as a source-owned path fact for later plbonus work, but should be curated against current headers before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Document unresolved matching tactics in fn_80039618 if still present
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2525 uses PAD_STACK(16) in fn_80039618 and a scoped dont_inline pragma around fn_80038700. Loaded evidence has no explicit rationale beyond the automated report, and fn_80039618 was reported at 99.67%. If these tactics remain in current source, curator may want a note or cleanup task tied to verifier evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards require evidence for PAD_STACK and pragmas; this PR slice shows their use but not the detailed justification.

rejection_notes: Do not promote fn_80039618 as a full match; the loaded automated report says 99.67%, not 100%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details; no human review comments or reviews were loaded.
Do not attribute the sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3AA7 report changes to specific source edits in this PR; those files were not changed in the loaded changed-file metadata.
Do not promote PAD_STACK(16) or dont_inline as general style from this PR alone; they need narrow verifier rationale under existing standards.
